expectations.text: HTML file generated: mindmap.html exists in outputs
expectations.evidence: File exists at outputs/mindmap.html with 485 lines of valid HTML containing embedded CSS and JavaScript that renders an SVG mindmap.

expectations.text: Connector visibility: Lines connecting nodes to center are clearly visible against dark background (not #2a2a4a or near-background)
expectations.evidence: Background is #0f0f1a (very dark). Core connector lines use category colors with 'bb' alpha suffix (e.g., #6366f1bb, #f472b6bb, #fb923cbb) at stroke-width 2.5. These are bright, saturated colors at ~73% opacity — all clearly visible against the dark background. None are #2a2a4a or near-background.

expectations.text: Cross-branch relationships: Inter-node dependencies shown as visual lines on the map, not just text descriptions
expectations.evidence: Lines 204-211 define 6 crossLinks (e.g., 코칭 AI → 운동 기록, 챌린지 → 소셜 기능). Lines 413-462 render them as SVG Bezier path elements with stroke '#94a3b880', stroke-dasharray '6 4', and midpoint labels. These are actual SVG lines drawn on the map between nodes, not just text descriptions.

expectations.text: Category color coding: Different branches use distinct colors with a legend
expectations.evidence: Legend div at lines 88-98 shows 8 categories with colored dots: #6366f1 (운동/트래킹), #f472b6 (식단/영양), #fb923c (소셜), #facc15 (챌린지), #4ade80 (AI 코칭), #22d3ee (웨어러블), #a78bfa (결제), #94a3b8 (인프라), plus a cross-link entry. Each branch uses its assigned color for node stroke, text fill, and connector stroke.

expectations.text: Central node present: Goal/project name at center with radial branch layout
expectations.evidence: Central circle at (CX=480, CY=480) with r=52 displays '피트니스 트래킹 앱' (Fitness Tracking App) as the goal. 9 branches are distributed radially using angle = startAngle + (2*PI*i)/totalBranches at CORE_RADIUS=200, with children at DETAIL_RADIUS=340. Classic radial mindmap layout.

expectations.text: CJK text rendering: Korean text fits within node boundaries without clipping
expectations.evidence: Core nodes: 110px wide, font-size 13px. Longest core text '웨어러블 연동' (7 Korean chars) at ~13px per char = ~91px, fits within 110px. Detail nodes: 95px wide, font-size 11px. Longest detail text 'i18n 프레임워크' mixes narrow Latin and Korean chars, estimated ~75px total, fits within 95px. All text uses text-anchor:middle for centering. System fonts (-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont) handle CJK well. No clipping is applied (no clip-path), and sizing is adequate for the text lengths used.

claims.claim: 8 core feature areas are covered in the mindmap
claims.evidence: The response.md claims '핵심 기능 영역 8개' but then lists 5 features and 4 infrastructure items (totaling 9 categories in the code). The categories array has 9 entries, not 8. The claim of '8' appears inaccurate — there are 9 category branches.
6 cross-links are defined and rendered as visual curved dashed lines with Korean labels (데이터 의존, 연동, 데이터 수집, 소셜 랭킹, 분석 데이터). These meaningfully represent inter-branch dependencies.

Lines 467-481 implement showTooltip/hideTooltip functions. Each node group has mouseenter/mouseleave handlers and dataset.tooltip attributes. A fixed-position tooltip div is styled and toggled via CSS class 'visible'.

suggestions.assertion: CJK text rendering: Korean text fits within node boundaries without clipping
suggestions.reason: This assertion is difficult to verify from HTML source alone — actual rendering depends on the user's system font metrics and browser. A stronger test would open the file in a browser and check for visual overflow, or measure SVG text bounding boxes programmatically.

No assertion checks whether the mindmap is actually renderable (e.g., no JavaScript errors, SVG viewBox is set correctly). The file could have a syntax error that prevents display. Consider adding a browser-based rendering check.
No assertion verifies that the state.md file was generated alongside the visualization. The state file (which was generated) is important for the planning compass workflow but goes unchecked.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the key visual and structural requirements well. The main gap is verifying actual rendered output rather than inspecting source code — CJK fitting and connector visibility would be more robustly checked via a headless browser screenshot.
